title: Lixiviant and uses thereof
abstract: A pyroligneous mixture comprising a combination of a pyroligneous liquor and a pyroligneous tar is disclosed. Also disclosed is a lixiviant composition comprising a combination of a pyroligneous liquor and a pyroligneous tar. The lixiviant composition may be used to extract metals from spent lithium ion batteries.
claims:
Claims 1. A pyroligneous mixture comprising a combination of a pyroligneous liquor and a pyroligneous tar. 2. A lixiviant composition comprising a combination of a pyroligneous liquor and a pyroligneous tar. 3. The pyroligneous mixture of claim 1 or the lixiviant composition of claim 2, wherein the pyroligneous mixture contains pyroligneous liquor and pyroligneous tar in a ratio of 1 : 1 to 15: 1. 4. The pyroligneous mixture or the lixiviant composition of claim 3, wherein the pyroligneous mixture contains about 10 wt%, about 16.7 wt% or about 25 wt% pyroligneous tar. 5. The pyroligneous mixture of any one of claims 1 to 4 or the lixiviant composition of any one of claims 2 to 4, wherein the pyroligneous mixture or the lixiviant composition consists essentially of a combination of a pyroligneous liquor and a pyroligneous tar. 6. Use of the pyroligneous mixture of any one of claims 1 to 5 or the lixiviant composition of any one of claims 2 to 5 as a lixiviant for extracting one or more metals from a substrate. 7. The use according to claim 6, wherein the substrate is a cathode active material from a lithium ion battery. 8. A process for extracting one or more metals from a substrate, the process comprising contacting the substrate with the pyroligneous mixture of any one of claims 1 to 5 or the lixiviant composition of any one of claims 2 to 5 under conditions to extract one or more metals from the substrate. 9. A process of preparing pyroligneous liquor and pyroligneous tar, the process comprising: pyrolyzing biomass, cardboard, paper, and/or lignocellulosic materials under conditions to generate a pyrolysis liquid; splitting the pyrolysis liquid into a pyroligneous liquor fraction and a pyroligneous tar fraction; treating the pyroligneous liquor fraction and the pyroligneous tar fraction after collection; and mixing the pyroligneous tar and the pyroligneous liquor in a desired ratio to generate a pyroligneous mixture. 10. A process of producing a pyroligneous mixture, the process comprising: 

 inputting a feedstock comprising biomass, cardboard, paper, and/or lignocellulosic materials into a pyrolysis reactor; purging the reactor with an inert gas; pyrolysing the feedstock under conditions to produce condensable and volatile gases; condensing the volatile gases in a condensing system; collecting a pyroligneous tar at a first condensation point of the condensing system; collecting a pyroligneous liquor at a second condensation point of the condensing system, wherein the pyroligneous tar and the pyrolysis liquid are collected separately from one another; and combining the pyroligneous liquor and pyroligneous tar in desired ratio to generate a pyroligneous mixture. 11. The process according to claim 10, further comprising selecting and/or treating the feedstock to a required low moisture threshold. 12. The process according to any one of claims 10 to 11, further comprising controlling the pyrolysis conditions to control the splitting of the pyrolysis liquid into the pyroligneous liquor fraction and the pyroligneous tar fraction. 13. The process according to any one of claims 10 to 12, further comprising configuring the condensing system to control splitting of the pyrolysis liquid into the pyroligneous liquor fraction and the pyroligneous tar fraction. 14. The process according to any one of claims 10 to 13, further comprising increasing the heating rate of the pyrolysis and/or lowering the total retention time of the biomass in the pyrolysis reaction to increase the proportion of pyroligneous tar fraction with respect to the pyroligneous liquor fraction. 15. The process according to any one of claims 10 to 14, further comprising increasing the heat exchange rate of the condensing system to increase the production yield of the pyroligneous tar and the pyroligneous liquor. 16. The process according to any one of claims 10 to 15, further comprising outputting the gas from the pyrolysis reactor to a first condensing unit at a controlled temperature. 17. The process according to any one of claims 10 to 16, further comprising treating the pyroligneous tar and pyroligneous liquor post-pyrolysis to configure the pyroligneous tar and pyroligneous liquor properties. 18. The process according to any one of claims 10 to 16, further comprising mixing pyroligneous liquor and pyroligneous tar in the ratio of 1:1 to 15:1, respectively.
